view.title: Trading Analytics
view.description: Deep statistical insights, performance curves, and strategy metrics
view.noTradesMatchDesc: Adjust your time range, select a different strategy or asset, or reset filters to view global stats.
view.noTradingRecordsDesc: Trading analytics requires recorded trade data. Log your trades, specify strategies, P&L amounts, and holding durations to unlock a full professional trading intelligence dashboard.

charts.title: Performance Charts
charts.description: Interactive visual analysis of your trading equity curves, session frequencies, and market skews
charts.winLossRatioDesc: Distribution of profit, loss, and BE trades
charts.monthlyPnlDesc: Net performance grouped by calendar months
charts.assetPairPnlDesc: Cumulative trade return ranked by symbols
charts.strategyReturnDesc: Cumulative trade P&L grouped by strategies used

insights.title: AI Performance Insights
insights.description: Pattern-based recommendations from your logged data
insights.generatingDesc: Log at least 2 trades with P&L information to unlock automated dynamic insights.
insights.assetOptimizationText: You perform exceptionally well on {{pair}}. It is your top asset, generating a net return of {{pnl}} over {{trades}} trades with an accurate win rate of {{winRate}}%. Consider focusing more on setups in this market.
insights.outstandingRiskText: Your average reward-to-risk ratio is {{ratio}}R. This is mathematically superior and guarantees that your account stays profitable even during brief drawdowns or low win-rate environments.
insights.rrRatioAlertText: Your average reward-to-risk ratio is currently {{ratio}}R. With an R-ratio below 1.0, your losing trades are bigger than your winners. Try squeezing more target yield out of high-confidence setups or setting tighter, structure-based stop losses.
insights.strongWinLossText: Superb scaling! Your average winning trade ({{avgWin}}) is {{ratio}}x larger than your average losing trade ({{avgLoss}}). This positive skewness represents professional risk-reward management.
insights.negativeDistributionText: Warning: Your average losing trade ({{avgLoss}}) is larger than your average winning trade ({{avgWin}}) by {{percent}}%. You are cutting winners too short or holding losers too long. Practice taking partial profits at targets and strictly cutting trades at stop-loss structure.
insights.marketFrictionText: You are experiencing friction trading {{pair}}, resulting in a net loss of {{pnl}} across {{trades}} trades. Consider lowering your trade size on this specific asset, checking for spread friction, or adapting strategy entry points.
insights.strategyAdaptationText: Your strategy "{{strategy}}" is currently underperforming with a total loss of {{pnl}} and a win rate of {{winRate}}%. Review whether market conditions (e.g. range vs. trend) are mismatching your setup parameters.
insights.drawdownResilienceText: You encountered a consecutive streak of {{streak}} losses. When entering a streak of 3+ consecutive losses, consider taking a 24-hour cooling-off period to prevent emotional trading (revenge trading) and preserve capital.
insights.viableSystemText: Your overall system expectancy is positive at {{expectancy}} per trade. Your trading rules and executions represent a mathematically sound edge over the market. Trust your system and stick to your strict execution rules.

advanced.title: Advanced Trading Metrics
advanced.description: Mathematical statistics assessing system expectancy, risk adjustments, and capital returns
advanced.expectancyDesc: The average amount of money you expect to make (or lose) per trade.
advanced.sharpeRatioDesc: Measures risk-adjusted performance. Above 1.0 is good, above 2.0 is excellent.
advanced.maxDrawdownDesc: The peak-to-trough drop in your equity curve — the largest dollar decline.
advanced.recoveryFactorDesc: Measures how quickly your account recovers from drawdowns (Total P&L / Max DD).
advanced.avgHoldingTimeDesc: The average duration of your trades, parsed from your logged journal entries.
advanced.roiDesc: Return on Investment computed as your cumulative P&L divided by total risked capital.
